text.title: Twitter/X Ad Creative and Copy
text.summary: Ad formats, specs, copy structure, and native vs polished creative rules.

list.item: Video — 0:15-2:20 duration, max 512MB; demos and engagement
Carousel — 2-6 images or videos; multiple products or steps
Text only — 280 characters; simple direct messages
App card — image + app install button; mobile apps

section.title: Copy Structure

rule.statement: Structure all Twitter/X ad copy as: Hook (stop scroll) → Value (what they get) → CTA (what to do) — never lead with brand name or product name.
rule.rationale: Twitter feed scrolls at high speed; the first line determines whether users stop. Brand-first openers are ignored; problem/benefit-first openers interrupt scrolling behavior.

section.reference: Three ad copy templates: problem/solution, social proof, value-first

rule.statement: Use conversational, text-heavy creative that looks like an organic tweet — avoid highly designed visuals with logos and taglines in the image.
rule.rationale: Twitter users have strong ad-blindness to polished promotional imagery. Native-looking ads blend into the timeline and receive 2-3x higher engagement rates than design-heavy formats on this platform.

list.item: Good: text-first, problem-aware, conversational tone, minimal branding in image
Bad: brand logo prominent, stock photo hero, marketing headline over image
